concept.title: About the DITA Package Processor
concept.shortdesc: Use the DITA Package Processor to run deterministic, auditable migration jobs across maps, topics, and media.

conbody.p: The DITA Package Processor is a migration engine for DITA packages created from mixed sources such as Word, HTML, Markdown, and generated XML. The engine keeps discovery, planning, and execution separate so you inspect intent before you apply filesystem changes.

ul.li: Scans package structure and relationships without mutation.
Normalizes discovery output into a planning contract.
Executes the plan in dry-run mode or apply mode.
Writes an execution-report.json with per-action outcomes, timing, and discovery context.

section.title: Why deterministic migration matters
section.p: When content quality varies across hundreds or thousands of files, you need predictable behavior. The processor does not hide decisions in handlers or runtime heuristics. You can review artifacts at each boundary:

ul.li: planning_input.json: what planning is allowed to use.

section.title: What the tool does not do

ul.li: It does not mutate files during discovery.
It does not infer custom business rules unless you implement a plugin.
It does not apply changes unless you pass --apply.
